# Test mcp-replay shadow functionality

# Create a test trace file with shadow responses
cat > shadow-trace.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1 compare=true
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1} # 1683000000.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"primary":true}}} # 1683000001.000
mcp-send-shadow {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"shadow":true}}} # 1683000001.100
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test","id":2} # 1683000002.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":"primary result"} # 1683000002.500
mcp-send-shadow {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":"shadow result"} # 1683000002.600
EOF

# Test 1: Default mode - should replay primary responses
exec mcp-replay -f shadow-trace.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv.*"method":"initialize"'
stdout 'mcp-send.*"primary":true'
stdout 'mcp-recv.*"method":"test"'
stdout 'mcp-send.*"primary result"'
! stdout 'mcp-send-shadow'

# Test 2: Mock server mode with primary responses (default)
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1}' | exec mcp-replay -mock-server -f shadow-trace.mcp
stdout '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"primary":true}}}'
! stdout '"shadow":true'

# Test 3: Mock server mode with shadow responses
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1}' | exec mcp-replay -mock-server -shadow -f shadow-trace.mcp
stdout '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"shadow":true}}}'
! stdout '"primary":true'

# Test 4: Auto-respond mode with shadow
exec mcp-replay -mock-server -auto-respond -shadow -f shadow-trace.mcp
stdout '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"shadow":true}}}'
stdout '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":"shadow result"}'
! stdout '"primary":true'
! stdout '"primary result"'

# Test 5: Test that shadow mode works with --json output
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test","id":2}' | exec mcp-replay -mock-server -shadow -json -f shadow-trace.mcp
stdout '"shadow result"'
! stdout 'mcp-send'

# Test 6: Test tracing with shadow mode
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1}' | exec mcp-replay -mock-server -shadow -trace trace-output.mcp -f shadow-trace.mcp
stdout '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"shadow":true}}}'

# Check trace file output contains shadow direction
exec cat trace-output.mcp
stdout 'mcp-send-shadow.*"shadow":true'
